title: Administration of Justice (Amendment) Ordinance 2024
text:
/**/

/**/

Administration of Justice (Amendment) Ordinance 2024

(assented to:    10 October 2024)

(published:       11 October
2024)

(commencement:  on publication)

AN
ORDINANCE

To amend
the Administration of Justice Ordinance 1949 to enable the waiver
of court fees in civil or family proceedings.

ENACTED
by the Legislature of the Falkland Islands ⎯

1.  Title

This
Ordinance is the Administration of Justice (Amendment) Ordinance
2024.

2.  Commencement

This Ordinance comes into force on publication in
the Gazette.

3.  Amendment of Administration of Justice
Ordinance 1949

This
Ordinance amends the Administration of Justice Ordinance
1949.

4.  New section 60A inserted

After
section 60, insert —

“60A.  Waiver of fees in civil or family
proceedings

(1)  The Head of Courts may, on application, waive a
civil or family court fee if satisfied that —

     (a)     one or
both of the qualifying criteria in subsection (2) are met;
and

     (b)     having
regard to all of the circumstances of the case, it is in the
interests of justice to waive the fee.

(2)  The qualifying criteria are —

     (a)     that
the person by whom the fee is payable is not reasonably able to
afford to pay it;

     (b)     the
proceedings in connection with which the fee is payable
—

(i)         
concern a constitutional issue, substantial question of law or
other matter of significant public interest; and

(ii)         are
unlikely to be commenced or continued unless the fee is
waived.

(3)  An application for the fee to be waived must be
made by the person liable to pay the fee in accordance with the
rules.

(4)  If the Head of Courts waives a fee after it has
been paid, they must refund the amount paid to the person who paid
it.

(5)  If the Head of Courts decides not to waive the
fee —

     (a)     the
applicant may apply to the Senior Magistrate to review the
decision; and

     (b)     the
Senior Magistrate may confirm, modify or reverse the
decision.

(6)  In this section —

“civil or family court fee” means a court fee
payable (whether under the rules or under any other enactment) in
connection with civil or family proceedings in any court, other
than —

     (a)     a fee
payable in connection with proceedings specified in the rules as
excluded proceedings;

     (b)     a fee
payable for proceedings in the Court of Appeal; or

     (c)     a fee
that became payable before this section commenced;

“Head of Courts” means the public officer appointed
as, or performing the duties of, Head of Courts and Tribunals
Service;

“rules” means rules made under section
60.”.

Passed by the Legislature of the Falkland
Islands on 26 September 2024.

CHERIE
YVONNE CLIFFORD,

Clerk
of the Legislative Assembly.

This
printed impression has been carefully compared by me with the Bill
which has passed the Legislative Assembly and is found by me to be
a true and correctly printed copy of the said Bill.

CHERIE
YVONNE CLIFFORD,

Clerk
of the Legislative Assembly.
